meta.description: Use this skill to run GSVA or ssGSEA pathway-level differential analysis from a bulk expression matrix and a sample group file, then generate a heatmap from the saved GSVA result object.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated DOI, PMID, p-values, or pathway enrichment scores across all 7 inputs; all values derived from actual GSVA/limma computation on provided data.

practice_boundaries.detail: Clinical diagnosis explicitly excluded in When Not to Use; privacy note for patient-linked data present; no medical recommendations made.

methodological_ground.detail: GSVA + limma is the canonical bulk pathway enrichment pipeline; method selection guide correctly advises on GSVA vs ssGSEA for different sample sizes and data characteristics.

code_usability.detail: All 9 R modules syntactically valid; dependencies (msigdbr, GSVA, limma, pheatmap) are standard Bioconductor/CRAN packages; three-mode branching logic is correct.

functional_suitability.note: Full coverage of GSVA, ssGSEA, limma differential analysis, heatmap generation, and result object reuse across three execution modes.

reliability.note: Hard stops on missing files; visualize mode guards against missing rda; append-only manifests preserve provenance across multiple runs in the same output_dir.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure via when-to-read table; references and algorithm details deferred; conditional mode execution avoids loading unnecessary analysis dependencies in visualize-only mode.

agent_usability.note: Mode selection guide provides genuine scientific guidance; execution model is explicit; method selection guide warns about sample size requirements; log messages at each step.

human_usability.note: Strong trigger keywords (GSVA, ssGSEA, KEGG, MSigDB); privacy note for patient-linked data is a best-practice addition; strict input validation is correct per Scene Override.

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; explicit privacy advisory for patient-linked matrices; file path validation; no prompt injection vectors.

maintainability.note: 9-module R architecture with clean separation; run_tests.R validates both analyze mode and visualize reuse in one pass; test data sourced from public GEO series.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision excellent with specific NOT-for exclusions and named alternative skills; rda object enables clean downstream composition; append-only manifests support idempotent multi-run workflows.

inputs.label: Full GSVA pipeline: bulk matrix vs Tumor/Healthy with KEGG gene sets
inputs.note: Full mode: load MSigDB KEGG -> GSVA scores -> limma diff analysis -> diff table + score matrices + rda + heatmap. All 8 expected outputs generated.

assertions.text: Output contains GSVA_diff.csv, GSVA_enrichment_results.csv, GSVA_list.rda, and heatmap PDF
assertions.note: All four primary outputs defined in output_items list in main.R and written by run_analysis_mode and run_visualization_mode.

assertions.text: limma logFC, P.Value, and adj.P.Val columns present in GSVA_diff.csv
assertions.note: GSVA_diff.csv column specification documented in Output Files section with all 7 required columns.

assertions.text: set.seed() applied before GSVA score computation
assertions.note: set.seed(opt$seed) called in main() before mode-specific execution branches.

assertions.text: No clinical diagnosis or treatment recommendation made
assertions.note: When Not to Use explicitly states clinical diagnosis and treatment selection are out of scope.

assertions.text: Privacy note for patient-linked data present in skill documentation
assertions.note: Input Validation section includes explicit anonymization recommendation for patient-linked matrices.

inputs.label: ssGSEA with MSigDB Hallmarks (H category), top 30 pathways
inputs.note: ssGSEA method with Hallmark gene sets; --top_n 30 applied to top pathway subset; GSVA_list.rda saved for downstream reuse.

assertions.text: ssGSEA method accepted and passed to GSVA computation function
assertions.note: --method ssgsea is a documented supported option; method selection guide confirms ssGSEA is appropriate for smaller sample sizes.

assertions.text: MSigDB Hallmark category (H) correctly retrieved from msigdbr
assertions.note: --category H is a valid MSigDB category; msigdbr supports H (Hallmarks) collection.

assertions.text: top_n=30 applied to GSVA_enrichment_results_topN.csv pathway subset
assertions.note: --top_n integer parameter documented; top pathway subset file documented in Output Files table.

assertions.text: GSVA_list.rda saved for downstream visualization reuse
assertions.note: data/GSVA_list.rda is always written in analyze and full modes per Output Files documentation.

inputs.label: GSVA with 3 samples per group — below recommended minimum
inputs.note: Analysis executes (no hard programmatic block for sample size in GSVA mode); method selection guide warns about minimum 10 samples/group for gsva method but does not emit a runtime warning. Minor gap identified.

assertions.text: Analysis runs without hard block for insufficient sample count in GSVA mode
assertions.note: No programmatic minimum sample count enforced; GSVA computation proceeds.

assertions.text: Method selection guide warns users about minimum sample sizes for gsva method
assertions.note: Method Selection Guide states: 'gsva: kernel-based enrichment scores suitable for... moderate-to-large sample sizes (>=10 samples per group recommended)'.

assertions.text: Runtime log_warn emitted when sample count falls below recommended minimum
assertions.note: No programmatic warning is emitted at runtime when sample count < 10 per group; user may proceed unaware of reliability concern.

assertions.text: Heatmap generated from top pathways when analysis completes
assertions.note: Visualization mode proceeds normally after analyze mode completes regardless of sample count.

inputs.label: Visualization reuse from saved GSVA_list.rda with custom heatmap parameters
inputs.note: Visualize mode loads rda without re-running analysis; top_up=10, top_down=10, top_mode=both applied; output appended to existing manifests.

assertions.text: Visualize mode loads GSVA_list.rda without re-running analysis
assertions.note: Mode branching in main.R: visualize mode calls run_visualization_mode only; SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND raised if rda missing.

assertions.text: top_up, top_down, top_mode parameters applied to heatmap pathway subset
assertions.note: All three parameters documented in Arguments table; subset logic implemented in plot_helpers.R/visualization.R.

assertions.text: SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND raised if GSVA_list.rda is missing from output_dir/data/
assertions.note: SKILL.md explicitly states: 'GSVA_list.rda must exist in output_dir/data/; run analyze or full mode first if it is missing'.

assertions.text: Output appended to existing output_manifest.txt and run_record.txt
assertions.note: Append-only manifest design confirmed: write_output_manifest and write_run_record called at end of every run.

inputs.label: Full GSVA with C2 REACTOME collection, FDR=0.01, top 50 pathways
inputs.note: Large REACTOME collection loaded from msigdbr; FDR=0.01 and top_n=50 applied; timeout parameter available for long runs.

assertions.text: C2/REACTOME gene sets loaded correctly from msigdbr using --subcategory CP:REACTOME
assertions.note: msigdbr supports C2 category with CP:REACTOME subcategory; query parameters passed through to function call.

assertions.text: FDR threshold 0.01 applied to top pathway selection
assertions.note: --fdr_threshold documented parameter; applied in top pathway subset generation.

assertions.text: top_n=50 applied to exported pathway score matrix
assertions.note: --top_n integer parameter; GSVA_enrichment_results_topN.csv contains top 50 pathways.

assertions.text: Timeout parameter available to bound long computation on large gene set collections
assertions.note: --timeout_seconds 0 default; user can set positive value to bound REACTOME computation time.

inputs.label: Single-cell RNA-seq pathway analysis request — out of scope
inputs.note: Out-of-scope response triggered; skill correctly declines and names the appropriate alternative workflow. No GSVA execution attempted.

assertions.text: Single-cell RNA-seq analysis explicitly identified as out of scope in When Not to Use
assertions.note: When Not to Use states: 'Single-cell RNA-seq clustering or communication analysis: use sc-clustering or cellchat'.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope response pattern provided with named alternative skill
assertions.note: SKILL.md includes the required out-of-scope response text with specific alternative skill names.

assertions.text: GSVA workflow not executed for single-cell request
assertions.note: Scope check precedes any file loading or computation per Execution Model step 1.

assertions.text: Named alternative skill (sc-clustering) referenced to help user proceed
assertions.note: sc-clustering referenced directly in When Not to Use section.

inputs.label: Sample names in group file do not match expression matrix columns
inputs.note: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH raised with actionable message identifying mismatched samples; no analysis proceeds with mismatched data.

assertions.text: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH raised when group file samples do not match matrix columns
assertions.note: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH error code documented: 'Sample names do not match between files'.

assertions.text: Error message identifies the mismatch clearly for agent-driven retry
assertions.note: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH error is structured and parseable; agent can diagnose and correct input alignment.

assertions.text: No partial GSVA analysis proceeds with mismatched sample data
assertions.note: Validation step 1 checks sample name alignment before any computation begins.

assertions.text: SKILL_* error code format enables automated retry with corrected inputs
assertions.note: Structured SKILL_* codes are the correct interface for agent-first execution per Scene Override.

key_strengths: Perfect static score: exemplary SKILL.md with complete documentation of all parameters, outputs, modes, and scientific method selection guidance.
Three-mode execution (analyze/visualize/full) with rda result object reuse is excellent workflow design enabling iterative visualization without expensive re-computation.
Explicit privacy note for patient-linked matrices is a best-practice addition that sets a standard for the skill collection.
Method selection guide (GSVA vs ssGSEA) provides genuine, actionable scientific guidance — not just parameter documentation.

recommendations.title: No runtime warning for low sample count in GSVA mode
recommendations.problem: When fewer than 10 samples per group are provided with --method gsva, the skill executes without any runtime warning. The method selection guide advises >= 10 samples but does not enforce this at runtime.
recommendations.root_cause: Sample count check exists conceptually in documentation but is not implemented as a programmatic log_warn in the analysis code path.
recommendations.fix: In run_analysis_mode, after loading and validating the group file, add: if (method == 'gsva' && min(table(group_labels)) < 10) log_warn('GSVA kernel method may be unreliable with fewer than 10 samples per group. Consider --method ssgsea for small cohorts.')

recommendations.title: Missing --help prerequisite note for optparse
recommendations.problem: The nomogram-construction sibling skill includes a note that --help requires optparse to be installed first. GSVA main.R also loads optparse at top level without this note in SKILL.md.
recommendations.root_cause: Cross-skill documentation consistency gap; note was added to nomogram but not GSVA.
recommendations.fix: Add a Prerequisites note to SKILL.md: 'R packages required: optparse, msigdbr, GSVA, limma, pheatmap, grid. Note: --help requires optparse to be loaded. Install optparse first if the package check fires before option parsing.'
